SMALL STREAMS AND CREEKS WELL OUT OF THEIR BANKS NEAR FAYETTE
6-10 COUNTY ROADS ACROSS THE COUNTY ARE UNDER WATER. CITY FIRE DEPARTMENTS ASSESSING LOCAL RIVERS/STREAMS. WATER NOT RUSHING OVER ROADS...JUST INUNDATING THEM.
WASHOUTS ON HIGHWAY 81. CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE TO BRIDGES, MUDSLIDES OCCURRED IN THE BEETOWN, BLOOMINGTON, LANCASTER AND ELLENBORO AREAS.

ROCK SLIDES AND ROAD CLOSURES REPORTED AROUND THE COUNTY FROM FLASH FLOODING OVERNIGHT. SAND BAGGING TAKING PLACE AT GARBER WITH EXPECTED RISES ON THE TURKEY RIVER.
AREA HIT HARD WITH WATER. SIX COUNTY ROADS WASHED OUT FROM ESTIMATED 5 TO 6 INCHES OF RAIN. ONE CAR STUCK IN HIGH WATER EARLIER ALONG BREMER-CHICKASAW COUNTY LINE.

FLASH FLOODING OVERNIGHT LED TO WATER OVER A DOZEN ROADS ACROSS THE COUNTY. TWO ROADS ARE STILL CLOSED THIS MORNING WHILE THE WATER HAS RECEDED FROM OTHER AREAS. THE LITTLE TURKEY RIVER AT WAUCOMA IS STILL RISING AND MAY FLOOD BY NOON. SAND BAGGING TAKING PLACE THERE.
COUNTY ROADS C AND X STILL CLOSED. MANY TOWNSHIP ROADS WERE FLOODED AS WELL. WORST HIT AREAS ARE SOUTH OF HIGHWAY 18. STREAMS AND SMALLER RIVERS ALL UP AND FLOODING.
